category_definitions.B: required but must be regenerated or revalidated
category_definitions.C: replaceable by installed dependencies after demonstrated equivalence
category_definitions.E: obsolete because no supported consumer remains

data_families.feature_status: Public Python calculator and website route are active.
data_families.provenance: Precomputed with Pandeia; exact engine/refdata versions and original builder are unresolved.

dependency_replacement.reason: Current code interpolates precomputed saturation/SNR grids. A direct Pandeia replacement has not been shown to preserve schema, values, units, or runtime.

data_families.current_schema: Top-level keys ta_snr, fullwell, mags, frame_time, sci_sat, ta_sat; five magnitude samples (4.5 through 12.5); instrument mappings for MIRI, NIRCam, NIRISS, and NIRSpec.
data_families.required_action: Recover or write a focused, version-recording generator and regenerate against the approved Pandeia 2026.7 engine/refdata; keep the small validated result packaged.
unresolved_questions: Can direct Pandeia calls replace any columns without unacceptable website latency?
evidence: Commit 686536ee added NIRSpec PRISM data after v1.2.6.3a

data_families.feature_status: Superseded in current code by the packaged input table.
data_families.provenance: 2020 precomputed table; engine/refdata versions unresolved.
data_families.current_schema: Same six top-level key families as the packaged table, but different content and checksum.
data_families.required_action: Retire from future external archives after updating the stale tutorial; do not remove the packaged table.
evidence: Repository-wide reader search found no current code reader of groups_integrations.json

data_families.feature_status: Active public API, website route, website smoke test, and unit test.
data_families.provenance: Fortney et al. (2010) model family; archive file dates to 2019. Exact database builder and input release are unresolved.
data_families.current_schema: SQLite: header plus 90 model tables; model rows contain id, pressure, wavelength, radius. Header records physical choices, units, reference radius/pressure, and citation.
data_families.required_action: Publish as an independently versioned optional forward-model component with its existing checksum until provenance or a validated successor is available.
unresolved_questions: Where is the original scientific builder?
Can the exact input model release be recovered?
evidence: Box and STScI members both SHA-256 24be915704c9aa22da3917f6aa0ea6f8c19921aa22b148c543ab08897e79d504

data_families.feature_status: Active public API, website route, website smoke test, and unit test.
data_families.provenance: Scientific builder and model-input provenance are unresolved.
data_families.current_schema: HDF5 groups catalog, spectra, wavelength. Catalog has 56,320 entries; each spectrum is float64[5000] with condensation, temperature, gravity, metallicity, C/O, haze, and cloud attributes; wavelength is float64[5000].
data_families.required_action: Keep unchanged as an optional forward-model component; do not reconstruct an uncertain builder in v2026.7.
unresolved_questions: Who generated the grid, with which code and opacity/model inputs?

data_families.feature_status: Active public API, website choice, documentation, and tests (tests use a small packaged grid).
data_families.provenance: PHOENIX-ACES-AGSS-COND-SPECINT-2011 files; local tree contains 1,056 scientific FITS models plus derived files.

dependency_replacement.reason: Pandeia supplies disk-integrated wavelength/flux SEDs in micron/mJy, not the required wavelength-by-mu specific-intensity arrays.

data_families.current_schema: Typical FITS primary array float64[78,25500], MU float64[78], ABUNDANCES[83], atmospheric parameters in PHX* headers. ModelGrid interpolates Teff, logg, Fe/H, mu, and wavelength.
data_families.required_action: Retain as a separately versioned optional limb-darkening component; validate archive cleanliness and provenance manifest.
unresolved_questions: Can a smaller lossless/scientifically equivalent representation be validated?
Are upstream on-demand downloads stable and licensed for this use?

data_families.feature_status: Active public API and default website model-grid choice.
tests: Small packaged ATLAS9 subset in modelgrid and limb-darkening tests
data_families.provenance: Kurucz ATLAS9 converted by exoctk.helpers.convert_ATLAS9; exact original input bundle is unresolved.

dependency_replacement.reason: The dependency API returns disk-integrated SEDs, not mu-dependent intensities.

data_families.current_schema: Typical FITS primary float64[17,1217], MU float64[17], ABUNDANCES[83], WAVELENGTH float64[1217], atmospheric PHX* headers.
data_families.required_action: Retain as a separate optional limb-darkening component; record original ATLAS9 inputs and converter version.
unresolved_questions: Which original ATLAS9 input files produced the 331 local FITS files?

data_families.display_name: ModelGrid pickle and HDF5 derived indexes/caches
data_families.feature_status: Load accelerators for active limb-darkening grids.
data_families.provenance: Generated by ModelGrid from local FITS paths; existing local ACES HDF5 is only a partial 2x2x3 cache and is not a full-grid release product.
data_families.current_schema: Pickled ModelGrid metadata plus HDF5 datasets flux[Teff,logg,FeH,mu,wavelength], mu[Teff,logg,FeH,mu], wave[wavelength].
data_families.required_action: Generate path-neutral, schema/versioned indexes only after the final grid layout is chosen; validate completeness against every raw FITS model.
unresolved_questions: Should full derived flux caches be distributed or built on demand?

data_families.feature_status: SUBSTRIP256 and SUBSTRIP96 contamination are exposed; SUBSTRIP96 intentionally reports only Order 1.
data_families.provenance: ExoCTK-generated Pandeia detector products; current files have no embedded Pandeia/code provenance.

dependency_replacement.reason: Pandeia supplies the source SED and detector calculation, but ExoCTK consumes generated 2-D trace templates; installed SEDs do not directly replace those FITS files.

data_families.current_schema: Per-temperature FITS with three float64 detector image HDUs for SOSS Orders 1, 2, and 3; current consumer applies a 1.5 observational scale.
data_families.required_action: Regenerate only after PRs #725/#726 merge and order-0 sign-off; embed code, Pandeia, refdata, SED, normalization, and schema provenance.
unresolved_questions: Which legacy trace set corresponds to which Pandeia/refdata version?

data_families.feature_status: Both DHS filters are active contamination modes on current main.
data_families.provenance: Local legacy installation contains candidate templates; they are absent from the directly inspected Box contamination archive and lack release provenance.
data_families.current_schema: Per-temperature NIRCam grism FITS with detector data shaped [1,51,1863]; consumer places it into ten DHS stripes.
data_families.required_action: Create a validated contamination component containing both filters after blockers clear; do not assume the unversioned local files are release-ready.
unresolved_questions: Where were the local F322W2/F444W templates published, if anywhere?
evidence: Box exoctk_contam.zip contains no F322W2/F444W directories

data_families.feature_status: Required by SOSS contaminant rendering; DHS uses a zero array.
data_families.provenance: Files were added operationally after v1.2.6.3a; no generator is present. Local tree has four temperature templates, an older generic template, and one galaxy template.
data_families.current_schema: Small NumPy 2-D arrays; source templates are selected by nearest temperature. calc_v3pa multiplies order 0 by 1.5e3 before source flux and empirical scaling.
data_families.required_action: Do not publish or regenerate until independent scientific calibration approves the absolute amplitude; then add generator and provenance.
unresolved_questions: What observational dataset establishes the absolute 1.5e3 scaling?
How were galaxy and temperature templates produced?

data_families.display_name: Legacy MIRI, NIRCam grism, NIRSpec, NIRISS, and DHS-F150W2 traces
consumers: Generic field_simulation/get_trace public API can still address APERTURES entries; the current web form marks NIRCam grism, MIRI, and NIRSpec as visibility-only.
data_families.feature_status: Not supported for contamination by the current web interface; removal is unsafe while the public field_simulation aperture contract remains broad.
data_families.provenance: Mixed legacy Pandeia/IDL products with no common manifest.
data_families.generator: make_contam_traces.py covers many but not all legacy layouts
archive_membership: legacy local installation; partly absent from current Box contamination archive
data_families.current_schema: Mixed FITS, IDL .sav/.pro, and auxiliary trace formats.
data_families.required_action: First make a focused support-contract decision. If Python API support is retained, regenerate and validate; otherwise remove code/API/docs/tests before retiring files.
unresolved_questions: Which non-web APERTURES are intentionally supported Python APIs?

data_families.feature_status: Optional mutable runtime accelerator; cache misses are computed and appended.
data_families.provenance: Generated from live ExoMAST/Gaia queries, current templates, pySIAF, visibility dates, and code; current schema records none of those versions.
data_families.current_schema: Per-target group attributes name, ra, dec, filled, goodPA_list; gzip float32 target_trace and sparse contamination; int16 plane_index.
data_families.required_action: Never distribute as immutable release data. Add schema/provenance keys and deterministic invalidation/migration before deployment regeneration.
unresolved_questions: What invalidation key should include query epoch/catalog endpoint and target alias resolution?

data_families.feature_status: Active mutable deployment state; tests create temporary databases.
data_families.provenance: Schema is fully defined in exoctk.log_exoctk.create_db; published copies differ in size/checksum and may contain mutable state.
data_families.current_schema: SQLite tables groups_integrations, limb_darkening, contam_visibility, phase_constraint, fortney, generic.
data_families.required_action: Remove from release archives; initialize and migrate an empty runtime database at deployment.
unresolved_questions: Does any deployed populated database require retention/migration outside release distribution?
evidence: Box DB SHA differs from STScI and local copies

data_families.feature_status: Active packaged inputs; 170+ configurations are exposed through JWST_THROUGHPUTS.
data_families.provenance: Repository history states these were generated from Pandeia refdata v1.5.2.

dependency_replacement.dimensions_units: Existing/generated tables are two columns: wavelength in micron and dimensionless total photon conversion efficiency; get_pce samples 500 wavelengths.
dependency_replacement.required_tests: For every exposed filename map its instrument/mode/filter/disperser/aperture, compare wavelength coverage, sampling, units, metadata, and PCE to the approved 2026.7 refdata within a reviewed tolerance.

data_families.current_schema: Two-column text sampled wavelength and throughput.
data_families.required_action: Regenerate or replace only in a focused PR after complete configuration/equivalence tests; retain packaged files meanwhile.
blockers: Current files trace to Pandeia refdata v1.5.2
unresolved_questions: Should runtime Pandeia become mandatory for all Throughput users?

data_families.display_name: Packaged contamination and visibility auxiliary tables
data_families.feature_status: Current packaged runtime/generation inputs.
data_families.provenance: Mixed: Gaia color relation and wavelength map provenance require documentation; target list is new on main; short ephemeris is legacy.

dependency_replacement.reason: Current new visibility plotting uses jwst_gtvt, but legacy visibilityPA still reads JWST_ephem_short.txt.

data_families.current_schema: Small text tables/lists with module-specific schemas.
data_families.required_action: Keep packaged; document provenance and split generation-only target list from runtime tables in the manifest.
unresolved_questions: Can legacy visibilityPA be retired in favor of jwst_gtvt?
